rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Embed explicit cell references and visible formulas in the spreadsheet (e.g., show formulas for ceded = MIN(MAX(...),...), retained, and LR in adjacent cells) so automated parsers can verify workbook formulas. Include a short statement clarifying inclusive/exclusive boundary convention at exact attachment/limit (e.g., whether loss == attachment results in ceded = 0). Add a short rounding / display precision note to explain % rounding to one decimal place.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Underwriting note documents the exact formula (ceded = MIN(MAX(gross - attachment,0), limit); retained = gross - ceded; retained LR = retained / premium). Numeric results in the note (ceded/retained/LR for 1-in-50, 1-in-100, 1-in-250) are arithmetically correct given inputs: attachment 25.0m, limit 50.0m, premium 9.8m. Calculations: 1-in-50 retained = 18.0m (LR 18/9.8 = 183.67%), 1-in-100 retained = 25.0m (LR 25/9.8 = 255.10%), 1-in-250 retained = 25.0m (LR 25/9.8 = 255.10%). Validation checks and a decision table tying recommendations to the numeric inputs are present in the underwriting note. The deterministic precheck confirms both required artifact files were produced. Note: the spreadsheet binary could not be parsed in this environment, but the artifact exists and the note + submitted key outputs are internally consistent.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Include explicit intermediate calculation rows and visible cell formulas in the spreadsheet (e.g., cells showing loss - attachment, min(..., limit) results) and ensure the workbook can be previewed programmatically (resolve openpyxl/runtime dependency) so automated graders can inspect formulas directly.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Underwriting note contains inputs, clear formula (recovery = min(max(loss-attachment,0), limit)), stepwise numeric results matching the reported retained losses and ratios (18M, 25M, 25M retained; 183.7%, 255.1%, 255.1%). Validation checks are listed and the decision table and recommendation (escalate) are present. Metadata and actual_outputs confirm arithmetic ties out and treaty capping behavior. The spreadsheet artifact exists and deterministic checks show no formula errors, though the workbook preview was unavailable in this environment.
